name: CISO Hub Metrics (Risk Posture, Compliance, Incidents)
description: Enterprise-tier customer-facing security posture dashboard: risk score, compliance status, incident register, CVE exposure, and a PDF board report.

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:2251-2265 (loadCisoMetrics()) correctly calls GET /api/ciso/metrics and correctly unwraps the {success,data,error} response envelope (its own comment at :2260-2262 explicitly documents doing this, unlike the systemic bug found elsewhere in this registry) — the frontend side of this integration is done right. The defect is server-side: workers/src/index.js:4300-4303 gates /api/ciso/metrics behind requireCan(authCtx, env, 'admin:business:read'), and workers/src/auth/rbac.js:101 defines that exact permission as isSuperAdmin-only ('business-critical, super-admin only', per its own inline comment) — satisfiable only by the platform owner, the ADMIN_KEY bypass, or a user with a granted SUPERADMIN role. No paying customer on any plan, including Enterprise/MSSP, can ever satisfy this. Because loadCisoMetrics() treats a non-ok response as 'no data' rather than an error (falling back to a zeroed default object silently), every real customer sees an all-zero CISO dashboard indefinitely, with no error message explaining why. By contrast, the sibling GET /api/ciso/posture route (workers/src/index.js:4307-4310) has no permission check at all and would work for any authenticated user. Independently confirmed 2026-07-08 by reading the permission definition, the route gate, and the frontend call site directly.

navigation.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:852 sidebar nav-item (data-page="ciso") reaches this page; frontend/index.html:19986-19991 also surfaces a homepage 'Executive Hub' widget that fetches the same endpoint.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file imports workers/src/handlers/cisoMetrics.js directly. No test asserts that a PRO/ENTERPRISE-tier (non-staff) customer can successfully call GET /api/ciso/metrics — the exact scenario that is currently broken.

verification.evidence: workers/src/auth/rbac.js:99-104 (PERMISSIONS registry) read directly; workers/src/index.js:4300-4310 read directly; frontend/user-dashboard.html:2251-2265 read directly and confirmed the envelope-unwrap is correct, isolating the defect to the server-side permission gate.

notes: A previously-uncatalogued, currently-live defect, not a wiring gap: the frontend is correctly built and correctly calls the real endpoint, but the endpoint's own access-control gate excludes the entire paying customer base it was built for. auth/rbac.js:101's own comment describes admin:business:read as intended for 'revenue/CRM/proposals — business-critical, super-admin only,' which strongly suggests this permission constant was reused on the customer-facing CISO route by mistake rather than by design, since the sibling /api/executive/ prefix route (CAP-DASH-002) correctly uses a tier-inclusive permission for a very similar audience. Recommended fix (not applied in this pass, since it changes an access-control boundary and deserves its own reviewed change): gate /api/ciso/metrics the same way the /api/executive/ prefix route gates itself — PRO/ENTERPRISE/MSSP tier OR platform admin — rather than SUPERADMIN-only.

name: Executive Hub / Command Center
description: Platform-wide MRR/ARR/paying-customer KPIs and a richer FAIR-risk/board-report Executive Command Center, for Enterprise-tier customers and platform admins.

frontend.evidence: frontend/index.html's cdbExecLoad() (around :19986-19991) calls GET /api/executive/dashboard as part of its 'Executive Hub' widget. That exact-match route (workers/src/index.js:5953-5958) is registered BEFORE the separate /api/executive/ prefix-dispatched route that backs handleExecutiveCommandCenter (workers/src/index.js:7726-7734), so the router serves it via handleGetDashboard, gated by requireCan(authCtx, env, 'admin:business:read') — the identical SUPERADMIN-only permission documented as broken for the customer base in CAP-DASH-001. No customer on any plan can load real data here either. In sharp contrast, the prefix-dispatched /api/executive/ block (workers/src/index.js:7726-7729) correctly allows any PRO/ENTERPRISE/MSSP-tier customer OR a platform admin (['PRO','ENTERPRISE','MSSP'].includes(tier) as an explicit OR-path around the RBAC check) — the two sibling 'executive' routes are inconsistently gated by two different rules, one customer-inclusive and one staff-only. Independently confirmed 2026-07-08 by reading both route blocks directly and confirming which one the frontend actually reaches first.

navigation.evidence: Embedded in frontend/index.html's homepage; cdbExecLoad() is the trigger function for this widget.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/executiveCommandCenterGate.test.mjs (run directly as part of this pass's full-suite check) tests the /api/executive/* catch-all's gating (anonymous rejected, FREE-tier rejected, ADMIN_KEY admitted) — but that test targets the prefix-dispatched handleExecutiveCommandCenter block, not the earlier exact-match /api/executive/dashboard route this widget actually calls, so it would not catch the defect described above.

verification.evidence: workers/src/index.js:5949-5958 and :7726-7734 both read directly to confirm dispatch order and confirm the two blocks use genuinely different gating logic, not a shared code path.

notes: Same root cause and same recommended fix as CAP-DASH-001 — align /api/executive/dashboard's gate with its own sibling /api/executive/ prefix route's tier-inclusive pattern, as a deliberate, reviewed access-control change rather than a drive-by fix in this registry pass.

name: Product & Growth Analytics (Funnel, Feature Adoption)
description: Internal growth-metrics API: conversion funnel, feature-adoption tracking, and event ingestion for product analytics.

frontend.evidence: Repo-wide search for /api/analytics/p3/ returns zero matches anywhere in frontend/. This handler's routes correctly use the tier-inclusive admin:analytics:read permission (workers/src/index.js:3168, satisfiable by a platform admin, unlike CAP-DASH-001/002's admin:business:read), but no page calls any of them. Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: No nav entry calls this handler's routes because no page does.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file imports workers/src/handlers/productAnalytics.js directly.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/productAnalytics.js exports enumerated; workers/src/index.js:3165-3195 route registrations and gate read directly; frontend/ searched exhaustively for any caller.

notes: Unlike CAP-DASH-001/002, this is a straightforward backend-only orphan (correct, sensible RBAC gate, simply no UI) rather than a broken-despite-correct-wiring case. The dashboard/role-personalization gap tracked by docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md's Domain 6 ('same finding as Domain 3 — no real personalization, only upsell overlays') is registered once, under docs/capability-registry/domains/rbac.json's CAP-RBAC-002, and not duplicated here.
